project.title: Human blood LC-MSMS Simple Protein Identification Report
project.projectDescription: The present study employs Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (LC-MS) technology for the identification of proteins, utilizing the Q-Exactive mass spectrometer produced by Thermo Fisher Scientific. Upon the analysis of the ARP1-USP13-OE sample via Proteome Discoverer software, a total of 3631 peptides were matched, corresponding to the identification of 690 distinct proteins. Subsequent analysis of the ARP1-WT sample with the same software revealed 4326 matched peptides, culminating in the identification of 760 proteins.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Main reagents and instruments:Formic acid (Fisher Scientific); Mass spectrometry purity;acetonitrile Fisher Scientific;sequencing grade trypsin (Trypsin);sep-Pak C18 desalting column,(Waters company);nano-upgrade peptide trapping column, Acclaim PepMap C18, 100 µm × 20mm (Thermo Scientific company);nano-upgrade peptide analysis column, Acclaim PepMap C18, 75µm × 250mm (Thermo Scientific).in-gel enzymatic hydrolysis,peptide segment desalting. Data processing protocol
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Q-Exactive LC-MSMS (Thermo) were used to analyze the quality of the treated samples,Proteome Discoverer 2.1 With the RAW file as the original file, the database corresponding to the species is selected, and then the database is searched and matched by the spectrum after PD extraction.

references.referenceLine: Guo M, Meng H, Sun Y, Zhou L, Hu T, Yu T, Bai H, Zhang Y, Gu C, Yang Y. Bruceine A Inhibits Cell Proliferation by Targeting the USP13/PARP1 Signalling Pathway in Multiple Myeloma. Basic Clin Pharmacol Toxicol. 2025 136(5):e70027
